title: Hawking-Page phase transition for pure Lovelock black holes
abstract: We investigate the thermodynamic properties of static, spherically symmetric Anti-de Sitter (AdS) black holes, focusing on the interplay between characteristic temperatures, as well as on the universality of Ruppeiner scalar curvature at the Hawking-Page (HP) phase transition. In particular, we study the relation between the minimum temperature and the HP phase transition temperature for static, spherically symmetric AdS black holes in pure Lovelock gravity. For the electromagnetically neutral case in Einstein gravity, the minimum temperature in $(d+1)$ dimensions coincides with the HP transition temperature in $d$ dimensions, while in higher pure Lovelock theories this relation is modified by a dimension- and order-dependent factor, reducing to the Einstein result in appropriate limits. For charged AdS black holes, in the grand canonical ensemble, in general relativity, the two temperatures differ by a simple dimension-dependent factor, whereas no universal relation persists in higher curvature pure Lovelock theories. We further analyze the normalized Ruppeiner scalar curvature at the HP transition and show that it is a universal constant depending only on the spacetime dimension for electromagnetically neutral black holes in pure Lovelock theories. The normalized scalar curvature remains a constant, under appropriate conditions, even for the charged static spherically symmetric black holes in the grand canonical ensemble for the Einstein theory case, whereas in general pure Lovelock theories it depends on thermodynamic parameters such as pressure and electrostatic potential, asymptotically approaching a constant in the large-pressure or simultaneous large-potential and large-pressure limits.
summary: This work analyzes the Hawking-Page phase transition for black holes in higher-curvature pure Lovelock gravity within AdS spacetime. It uses advanced thermodynamic geometry, specifically the Ruppeiner scalar curvature, to probe universality across different dimensions and charges. The findings reveal that while simple dualities hold in General Relativity, these universal relationships are modified or broken by higher-order curvature terms.
main_problem: The study investigates the thermodynamic properties and phase structure of Anti-de Sitter (AdS) black holes governed by pure Lovelock gravity, focusing on the Hawking-Page (HP) phase transition.
main_result: The normalized Ruppeiner scalar curvature at the HP transition is shown to be a universal constant depending only on spacetime dimension for neutral cases. The simple dual relationship between minimum temperature and $T_{HP}$ breaks down in higher pure Lovelock theories.
method: Thermodynamic analysis, utilizing the grand canonical ensemble, calculating Gibbs free energy ($G$), and employing geometric tools like the Ruppeiner scalar curvature to diagnose phase transitions.
model_or_system: Static, spherically symmetric AdS black holes described by pure Lovelock gravity in $d$ dimensions. The analysis extends to include electric charge ($\Phi$) and varying the Lovelock order ($n$).
key_observables: Hawking temperature minimum ($T_0$), Hawking-Page transition temperature ($T_{HP}$), Gibbs free energy ($G$), and normalized Ruppeiner scalar curvature ($R_N$).
important_parameters: Spacetime dimension ($d$), Lovelock order ($n$), electrostatic potential ($\Phi$), and thermodynamic pressure ($P$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the grand canonical ensemble description and relies on interpreting $G=0$ for the HP transition. Approximations are used in analyzing joint limits of $\Phi$ and $P$.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate plots showing the dependence of the normalized Ruppeiner scalar curvature ($R_N$) on electrostatic potential ($\Phi$) and pressure ($P$), demonstrating plateaus and sign changes at the HP transition.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the phase structure by comparing $T_0$ (local minimum) with $T_{HP}$ (global free energy crossing). It then uses geometric diagnostics like $R_N$ to characterize the universality of this transition across different Lovelock orders and charge regimes.
why_interesting: While focused on gravity, the use of Ruppeiner geometry and phase transition analysis directly relates to statistical mechanics concepts of critical points and thermodynamic stability in complex systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The above figure shows the plots for the Gibbs free energy vs temperature. In panels F1, F2, F3, and F4, the colours {Red, Blue, Green} represent the potential Φ with values {0, 0.25, 0.7}. In figures F5 and F6, the colours {Orange, Cyan, Black} represent pressure values of {0.7, 0.5, 0.3}.

Figure 2: The above plots depict the dependence of the normalized Ruppeiner scalar curvature on the electric potential Φ and pressure P. In panels F1, F2, F3, F4 the pressure is kept constant. Solid Colour plots with color {Red,Blue, Green} represent pressure {27,23, 17} while the dashed plots with color {Red, Blue, Green} have pressure {10,7,5}. In panels F5, F6 the electric potential is fixed with colors {Orange,Cyan, Black} representing electric potential {0.03,0.1, .15}. The pink dashed curves in F3 and F4 describe the constant electrostatic potential at which RN of the red curves changes sign from negative to positive.
